name: OpenVINO
pricing_notes: Open source (Apache 2.0); Intel product; no licensing costs

auth.notes: Local inference library; no auth required

performance.notes: 3-10x faster inference than baseline on Intel CPUs; significant speedup on Intel iGPU/NPU; model-dependent

agent_readiness.notes: Intel's toolkit for optimizing and deploying neural networks on Intel hardware (CPU, integrated GPU, Neural Processing Unit). Converts models from PyTorch/TensorFlow/ONNX to OpenVINO IR format. INT8 quantization, post-training optimization. Tokenizers and GenAI pipeline integration for LLM inference. Particularly strong on Intel Arc GPUs and AI PC NPUs. Used in edge AI and CPU inference deployments.

gotchas: Intel hardware strongly preferred — AMD/NVIDIA performance gains minimal
Model conversion step required (PyTorch → OpenVINO IR)
Not all PyTorch operations supported — dynamic shapes require special handling
LLM support improving but less mature than CUDA/llama.cpp
best_when: CPU-only inference deployments (no GPU) on Intel processors
Edge AI on Intel hardware (industrial, IoT, laptop NPU)
INT8 quantization for reducing model size and improving CPU throughput
Computer vision inference (object detection, segmentation) on Intel CPUs
avoid_when: AMD or Apple Silicon hardware (use llama.cpp or MPS)
summary: OpenVINO is Intel's open-source toolkit for optimizing and deploying neural networks on Intel hardware. Provides 3-10x CPU inference speedup via INT8 quantization and graph optimization. Supports PyTorch/TensorFlow/ONNX model conversion. Strong for edge AI and CPU-only deployments on Intel processors. Includes GenAI pipelines for LLM inference. Best for Intel hardware environments requiring maximum inference throughput.
